summary: The reproduction bundle starts Langflow from the pinned vulnerable source checkout, obtains an unauthenticated bearer token from /api/v1/auto_login, and then proves backend command execution by reflecting a command-generated proof string through /api/v1/custom_component.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should obtain a bearer token from /api/v1/auto_login and then make /api/v1/custom_component return the exact command-generated proof string EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF from attacker-supplied Python executed on the server.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the pinned Langflow commit, built the helper image, started Langflow from the mounted source checkout, obtained a bearer token from /api/v1/auto_login, and then made /api/v1/custom_component return display_name=EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF from attacker-supplied Python that executed /bin/sh on the server.
verification_method: The helper starts Langflow from the mounted repository source, polls /api/v1/auto_login until it receives a bearer token, submits custom-component code whose module-level subprocess invocation prints EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF, and verifies that the returned component display_name exactly matches the proof string. That response can only occur if the backend executed the attacker-controlled Python.
evidence_produced_by_run: The reproduction printed: [evohunt] auto_login returned a bearer token.
The reproduction printed: [evohunt] SUCCESS: observed attacker-controlled command output EVOHUNT_RCE_PROOF.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
